inline_comments.message: Can you explain a little, how it works to have 2 identical entries here and in "gerrit-maven-docker-stage" to generate the 2 builds?  Is there a wiki page that I could look at to get a little more background?  Thank you.

The entries have different project name, one for amd64, the other for arm64. This was done in order to use the same template for both jobs, but without modifying the original template. The main reason was for the staging job, which is in global jjb and it would be difficult to modify it upstream. So the same approach was used for both merge and staging jobs. 

https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Migration+to+DockerHub describes what has been done for the multiarch effort. I will add the reasoning to this approach in the documentation there.
This is not in the amd64 version, oversight? Could this be why the arm64 takes only a minute to run while the amd64 takes over 20 minutes? At least we are seeing that on the policy/docker jobs.
interestingly, it's the reverse: arm64 takes 20 minutes, while amd64 takes only one (for the step, "Generating Requirements File")
